# Copilot instructions for this repository

- **Stack:** Ruby, Ruby on Rails (library).
- **Packages to respect:** actioncable, actionmailbox, actionmailer, actionpack, actiontext, activejob, ….
- **Package manager:** bundler.
- **Files:** snake_case .rb files mirroring module/class hierarchy (e.g. action_cable/channel/base.rb, action_cable/server/worker/active_record_connection_management.rb); JavaScript files in app/javascript/action_cable/ use snake_case as well
- **Components:** PascalCase Ruby modules and classes nested under their gem namespace (ActionCable::Channel::Base, ActionCable::Server::Broadcasting); mixins extracted as modules with descriptive noun names (Callbacks, Streams, Naming, Broadcasting, Identification)
- **Functions / variables:** snake_case Ruby methods; private helpers suffixed with intent (e.g. worker_pool_stream_handler, serialize_broadcasting, channel_with_prefix); predicate methods end with ? (subscription_rejected?, alive?, stopping?); bang methods end with ! (clear_action_methods!, defer_subscription_confirmation!); snake_case instance variables with @-prefix matching their reader name (@server, @worker_pool, @pubsub); local variables snake_case; constants ALL_CAPS or PascalCase inside VERSION/INTERNAL hashes
- **Layout:** Each Rails component (actioncable, actiontext, activestorage) is a self-contained gem directory with lib/<gem_name>.rb as the entry point and lib/<gem_name>/ holding sub-modules mirroring the Ruby namespace. Test files live under test/ inside each component directory. JavaScript source for actioncable lives under actioncable/app/javascript/action_cable/ and tests under actioncable/test/javascript/. Generators, templates, and Rails integration files are placed under lib/rails/ and are explicitly ignored by Zeitwerk.
- **Errors:** Errors are raised as specific StandardError subclasses defined inline within the relevant module (e.g. Authorization::UnauthorizedError, NonInferrableChannelError, RemoteConnection::InvalidIdentifiersError); rescue_with_handler from ActiveSupport::Rescuable is used in Channel::Base#dispatch_action and Connection::Base to delegate exception handling to user-defined rescue_from blocks, with bare Exception rescues in worker/event-loop contexts that log and close rather than propagate.
- **State:** Long-lived state is held in instance variables on Connection::Base and Channel::Base objects (not re-created per request); lazy singleton resources on Server::Base (@pubsub, @worker_pool, @event_loop) are initialized inside @mutex.synchronize blocks using the ||= pattern; channel subscription state (streams, confirmation flags) uses Concurrent::AtomicFixnum for thread-safe counters.
- **Tests (Minitest):** Tests use Minitest (ActiveSupport::TestCase subclasses) located in each component's test/ directory; channel functional tests subclass ActionCable::Channel::TestCase which provides subscribe/perform/unsubscribe helpers and assert_has_stream/assert_broadcasts assertions; ConnectionStub and ChannelStub are injected to isolate WebSocket I/O; JavaScript tests in actioncable/test/javascript use QUnit with a testDone hook to restore ActionCable.adapters.WebSocket after each test.
- **Pattern `actioncable/lib/action_cable/channel/callbacks.rb` (ActiveSupport::Concern mixin modules):** Encapsulate a single cross-cutting behaviour (Callbacks, Streams, Naming, Broadcasting, Identification, PeriodicTimers) and include it into Base classes; ClassMethods sub-module pattern used throughout
- **Pattern `actioncable/lib/action_cable.rb` (Zeitwerk autoloading with explicit ignores):** Gem entry point configures Zeitwerk::Loader.for_gem, ignoring generators, version files, and test helpers so they are not eager-loaded; adapters are do_not_eager_load and required on demand
- **Pattern `actioncable/lib/action_cable/channel/base.rb` (ActiveSupport::Notifications instrumentation):** Wrap significant operations (perform_action, transmit, broadcast, transmit_subscription_confirmation) in instrument calls with a dotted-name payload so subscribers can hook in metrics/logging without modifying core code
- **Pattern `actioncable/lib/action_cable/server/base.rb` (Mutex-guarded lazy singleton initialisation):** Server resources (@pubsub, @worker_pool, @event_loop, @remote_connections) are created on first access inside @mutex.synchronize { @ivar ||= ... } to be thread-safe without paying the cost of locking on every access
- **Pattern `actioncable/lib/action_cable/subscription_adapter/base.rb` (SubscriptionAdapter base + adapter-per-file pattern):** SubscriptionAdapter::Base defines the interface (broadcast/subscribe/unsubscribe/shutdown raise NotImplementedError); concrete adapters live in separate files under subscription_adapter/ and are required on demand via pubsub_adapter in Configuration
- **Pattern `actioncable/lib/action_cable/channel/test_case.rb` (TestCase Behavior module with stub objects):** Test infrastructure is packaged as an includable Behavior module inside a TestCase class; ConnectionStub and ChannelStub replace real WebSocket objects so channel logic can be exercised in plain Minitest without a running server
- **Pattern `actioncable/lib/action_cable/engine.rb` (ActiveSupport.run_load_hooks / on_load deferred configuration):** Components register load hooks at the bottom of their Base files (run_load_hooks :action_cable_channel) and the Engine wires configuration via on_load blocks, keeping framework integration decoupled from the library core
- **Pattern `actioncable/lib/action_cable/server/worker.rb` (Worker async_invoke / async_exec thread-pool dispatch):** All channel action and connection callback execution is dispatched through Server::Worker#async_invoke onto a Concurrent::ThreadPoolExecutor, keeping the event-loop thread free; exceptions are caught, logged, and handle_exception is called if available
- **Avoid:** Don't call channel action methods directly on a Channel::Base instance in tests — always go through subscribe + perform so ChannelStub intercepts streams and the subscription lifecycle runs correctly
- **Avoid:** Don't add public methods to a Channel subclass that are not intended to be client-callable actions — every public instance method becomes part of action_methods and is remotely invocable
- **Avoid:** Don't bypass the worker pool by executing blocking work directly on the event-loop thread (e.g. inside stream callbacks) — use connection.worker_pool.async_exec instead
- **Avoid:** Don't require subscription adapter files eagerly or at load time — adapters are intentionally do_not_eager_load and must be required on demand via pubsub_adapter to avoid loading unused adapter dependencies
- **Avoid:** Don't mutate Server::Base singleton resources (@pubsub, @worker_pool) outside of @mutex.synchronize — concurrent initialization without the mutex will cause race conditions
- **Avoid:** Don't define subscription adapter logic outside of the subscription_adapter/ directory or skip the ChannelPrefix module when implementing a new adapter that needs channel namespacing
- **Avoid:** Don't restore ActionCable.adapters.WebSocket manually in JavaScript tests — the QUnit testDone hook in test_helpers/index.js already handles restoration; adding a second restore will conflict
- **Common task (Adding a new ActionCable channel with tests):** 1. Create lib/action_cable/channel/<name>.rb defining a module ActionCable::Channel::<Name> (or place in the consuming app under app/channels/). 2. Subclass ActionCable::Channel::Base and include any needed concern modules (Streams, Broadcasting, PeriodicTimers) — most are already included via Base. 3. Implement subscribed (and optionally unsubscribed) lifecycle callbacks; call stream_from or stream_for inside subscribed; call reject if authorization fails. 4. Declare public action methods for client-callable RPCs; keep private helpers private to prevent unintended remote invocation. 5. If the channel should broadcast to model-scoped queues, use broadcast_to / broadcasting_for from the Broadcasting concern rather than constructing strings manually. 6. Create test/<component>/channel/<name>_test.rb subclassing ActionCable::Channel::TestCase. 7. In the test, call stub_connection(user: ...) to set connection identifiers, then subscribe(params) to instantiate the channel with ChannelStub injected. 8. Assert subscription state with assert subscription.confirmed? / subscription.rejected?, stream presence with assert_has_stream / assert_has_stream_for, and transmitted messages via transmissions. 9. Test action dispatch with perform :action_name, data_hash and assert on transmissions.last or use assert_broadcast_on for pubsub broadcasts. 10. Run the component's test suite with ruby -Itest test/channel/<name>_test.rb (or the full suite via the component's Rakefile) to verify.
